title: SILVIA: Sovereign Information Learning Virtual Intelligent Architecture
description: A federated Digital Twin framework for sovereign territorial data governance. SILVIA converts qualitative territorial observations into structured ecological knowledge graphs using a modular community-based architecture and the Territorial Regeneration Index (TRI), a composite metric integrating ecosystem diversity, cultural integrity, regeneration capacity, and information sovereignty. Designed to bridge ISO 19650 digital twin standards with low-connectivity environments where communities monitor their own territories.
references: Berkes, F. (2008). Sacred Ecology: Traditional Ecological Knowledge and Resource Management. Routledge.
Mang, P., & Reed, B. (2012). Designing from place: a regenerative framework and methodology. Building Research & Information, 40(1), 23-38.
Niemi, G. J., & McDonald, M. E. (2004). Application of ecological indicators. Annual Review of Ecology, Evolution, and Systematics, 35, 89-111.
CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance. Global Indigenous Data Alliance (GIDA).
